owasp: A01:2021 Broken Access Control (BOLA) / API1:2023 (also A07 broken authentication — see note)
surface: localhost XBEN-003-24, GET /dashboard trusting client-supplied X-UserId header with no ownership/session-binding check

oracle_verdict.note: The dashboard route trusts a client-supplied X-UserId header to resolve identity with only an existence check (User.query.get), never binding it to the authenticated session, so any caller who guesses another account's id (here, a second seeded account holding the flag) gets that account's full profile — confirmed both gray-box (source-located id) and pure black-box (blind header-value fuzz), converging on the same id and flag.

review_history.note: two independent methods (gray-box source-located id, pure black-box blind fuzz) converged on the same id and byte-identical flag; black-box replay additionally showed the header alone bypasses session/login entirely

evidence_contract.justification: Two independently-dispatched Hunters using two different methods (one consulting the benchmark's own gray-box source to locate the target id, the other purely black-box brute-forcing the header value) converged on the identical id (10052) and byte-identical flag; the source itself never contains the literal flag value (it reads /flag at container runtime), so the gray-box method located structure, not the answer, and the app was still required to serve it live.
